problem_text_cn: 1411 以$\lambda = 200\ \mathrm{nm}$的紫外光照射一清洁的金属表面，测得光电子的遏止电压是$2.6\ \mathrm{V}$。试问这种金属的功函数是多少？当改用$\lambda = 300\ \mathrm{nm}$的紫外光照射时，遏止电压是多少？若用可见光照射，情况又将如何？（$hc = 1240\ \mathrm{eV \cdot nm}$）
problem_text_en: 1411 Ultraviolet light with $\lambda = 200\ \mathrm{nm}$ irradiates a clean metal surface, and the stopping voltage of photoelectrons is measured to be $2.6\ \mathrm{V}$. What is the work function of this metal? What is the stopping voltage when ultraviolet light of $\lambda = 300\ \mathrm{nm}$ is used instead? What happens if visible light is used? ($hc = 1240\ \mathrm{eV \cdot nm}$)
problem_solution_cn: 【核心思路】利用爱因斯坦光电效应方程$E_{\text{kmax}} = h\nu - \phi_0 = eV_s$，先由已知条件求出功函数$\phi_0$，再代入不同波长计算遏止电压，最后判断可见光是否能产生光电效应。
problem_solution_en: 【Core Idea】Use Einstein's photoelectric effect equation $E_{\text{kmax}} = h\nu - \phi_0 = eV_s$. First, calculate the work function $\phi_0$ from the given conditions, then substitute different wavelengths to find the stopping voltage, and finally determine if visible light can produce the photoelectric effect.
problem_answer: 金属的功函数为$\boldsymbol{\phi_0 = 3.6\ \mathrm{eV}}$；改用$\lambda = 300\ \mathrm{nm}$的紫外光时，遏止电压为$\boldsymbol{V_s = 0.53\ \mathrm{V}}$；可见光（波长$>400\ \mathrm{nm}$，光子能量$<3.1\ \mathrm{eV}$）的能量小于金属的功函数，**不能产生光电效应**。
